title: gap-question-phraser output
description: 0 to 3 founder-facing gap questions, one per selected criterion, in the order the criteria were supplied. This shape is FROZEN: it is rendered verbatim by the built frontend as gap_questions[] (lovable-brief.md §4.1) and as questions[] in the follow-up contract (§4.4). Do not add fields.

criterion_id.description: Echo of the supplied criterion id. Against the live seeded config only L2, L3 and X5 are ever selected — the other founder-score criteria are reachable by public sources and are never asked.

question.description: Past-tense, single ask, Mom Test register. A sufficient answer contains a name, a number or a date. Exactly one question mark. Must contain no forward-looking framing and none of the forbidden stems.
examples: Who is using it today, and how did the first one find you?

why.description: One line naming what we could not learn on our own. When card_context.deck_readable is false it must NOT claim the deck omitted anything.
examples: Your deck doesn't name a first customer or pilot.

placeholder.description: Rendered on the textarea. States the smallest sufficient answer; never implies length or polish is rewarded.
examples: A name, a date, and how the conversation started is enough.

examples.question: Who is using it today, and how did the first one find you?
examples.why: Your deck doesn't name a first customer or pilot.
examples.placeholder: A name, a date, and how the conversation started is enough.

examples.question: Think of the last person who really wanted this — what were they using before, and what made them start looking?
examples.why: Your deck says "logistics companies", which doesn't tell us who actually signs.
examples.placeholder: Their job title, their company size, and what they did instead is plenty.

examples.question: When someone chose a different tool over yours, what did they pick and what did it do better?
examples.why: We can find who your competitors are; we can't find where you actually lose.
examples.placeholder: One specific instance is more useful than a full comparison.
